title: Jessie Buckley Wins Golden Globe for Best Actress in a Drama
text: Jessie Buckley claimed the Golden Globe for best actress in a drama at the 83rd Annual Golden Globes on January 11, 2026, for her role in Hamnet. This victory marks a continuation of Buckley's award season success. She previously won the Critics Choice Award for the same performance and earned a Screen Actors Guild nomination. Buckley competed against Jennifer Lawrence (Die My Love), Renate Reinsve (Sentimental Value), Julia Roberts (After the Hunt), Tessa Thompson (Hedda), and Eva Victor (Sorry, Baby). This was her first Golden Globe nomination. During her remarks, Buckley expressed her surprise at the moment, stating: This is not a normal feeling or situation to be in. She thanked Focus Features and the film's supporters, then highlighted the crew's contributions. She gave special recognition to the film's key grip, Tomasz Sternicki, sharing a memorable detail: he brought a cast iron pot from Poland to set and prepared soup for the crew using potatoes, onions, and meat. Directed by Oscar winner Chloé Zhao, the film stars Buckley as Agnes Shakespeare alongside Paul Mescal. The picture earned six Golden Globe nominations total, with Hamnet winning best motion picture drama.
